_placement_note: kot-assess/1 record per docs/next/research-engine.md SS2.1, at registry/assessments/<id>.json alongside f2b-replicate and compression-census. This interprets a Tier-0 DETERMINISTIC COUNTING census (bead kernel-of-truth-8es lineage), not a frozen experiment: there is NO frozen SAP and NO verdict object. Backing is the honesty-guard tooling-measurement rail (committed RUN-LOG + reproduce command + shard shas). The RUN-LOG asserts MEASURED counts only and explicitly forbids extrapolation to any benchmark (WiC / def-MMLU / kappa_B); the interpretation of what those counts MEAN is decided here.
assessor_binding: FABLE interpretive assessment per docs/next/opus-execution-practices.md practice 4 and the kern Fable/Opus execution split: Opus ran define_census.py and reported mechanical counts + a diagnosed mechanical cause; what the coverage MEANS for the grounding thesis, the linter Axis-A, and N1-LB kappa_B is decided here and only here. One of 4 concurrent feasibility-push agents; scope = registry/assessments ONLY.

measurement_refs.population: 17,211 minted logicalDefinition-bearing onto-obo concepts (GO 9,307 + SO 219 + MONDO 7,685)
measurement_refs.engine: tools/axiom/kot_axiom.py DEFINE op (genus-differentia lookup), post bead 8es (extractor resolves differentia relations + engine reads the resolved relation field) + endorsements loaded from data/axioms-definitional-v0/

_surprise_note: No prereg claim.md / registered prior exists (Tier-0 substrate census), so no surprise-* tag is licensed. Informally this is the EXPECTED pattern predicted verbatim by coverage-growth-ingestion-plan.md: (i) the genus-differentia define-op is 'the cheapest kappa_B lever ... it needs zero new ingestion' and GO's 9,303 logical defs were the named ready substrate (GO measured 1.0); (ii) SS0 'checkability is not vocabulary coverage — three legs must coincide'; (iii) MONDO's foreign fillers (HP/CHEBI/NCBITaxon) are precisely the Wave-A ingestion dependency held for later. All three predictions held; the on-trajectory confirmation is captured in interpretation, not a surprise flag.

interpretation.headline: 0.7710 internal define-checkability is a strong Axis-A SUBSTRATE-maturity signal (the cheapest kappa_B lever now demonstrably works: GO + SO fully checkable, MONDO's residual is a cleanly-diagnosed DATA-ingestion gap, not an engine limit) — and it is NOT a kappa_B number and NOT an end-task grounding claim. It measures at most two of the three checkability legs (record present + define-op resolves) on the SELF-AUTHORED minted substrate; the binding third leg (mapper parse of an external benchmark item) is untouched. This is exactly the pattern the ingestion plan predicted: ingestion delivers Axis-A now; kappa_B waits on leg-3 + benchmark-data unblock.
interpretation.measured_read: MEASURED post-8es: 13,270/17,211 define-checkable = 0.7710 (up from 0.5478 pre-8es). By shard: GO 9,307/9,307 = 1.0000 (byte-unchanged by 8es); SO 219/219 = 1.0000 (8es fully unlocked it from 18/219 = 0.0822); MONDO 3,744/7,685 = 0.4871 (from 103/7,685 pre-8es). The 3,941 MONDO ERR_DEFN_UNRESOLVED are ALL foreign fillers (HP / CHEBI / NCBITaxon references), i.e. the held Wave-A cross-ontology ingestion gap — deterministically diagnosed, 0 failures on unminted genus/filler. The pre-8es cause (differentia-relation shorthand absent from the 10-value alias table) is now retired: 8es (extractor resolving relations + the engine reading the resolved relation field) was the lever, not the DEFINE engine, which was never the bottleneck.
licenses: [MEASURED] Axis-A substrate maturity: post-8es the genus-differentia DEFINE op — the plan's named 'cheapest kappa_B lever' — resolves 77.10% of the minted onto-obo definitional substrate into engine status=answer, WITH GO AND SO COMPLETE (1.0 each). For the linter's Axis-A consumer (proposition-level coverage: each clean definitional record moves a proposition from lattice class U toward G+/G- on its covered domain, coverage-growth-ingestion-plan.md SS1), the substrate is 77% live and the define-op lane is functional end-to-end on ingested records. This is the records-only Axis-A value the plan says ingestion delivers immediately, now MEASURED rather than asserted.

[MEASURED] The residual is a DATA gap with a NAMED mechanical route, not an engine defect: all 3,941 MONDO misses are foreign fillers (HP/CHEBI/NCBITaxon) whose target shards are un-ingested. The Wave-A ordered runlist (A4 HPO, A6 ChEBI, A7 NCBITaxon; coverage-growth-ingestion-plan.md SS5) is exactly the route that would resolve them. GO byte-unchanged + SO fully unlocked confirms the engine + alias table are complete where their referents are already ingested.

[MEASURED] 8es was the decisive lever: define-checkability moved 0.5478 -> 0.7710 from the relation-resolution change alone, with SO going 0.0822 -> 1.0 and MONDO 0.0134 -> 0.4871. This is a clean instrument-improvement datum: the pre-8es number under-measured the substrate because of a shorthand-alias gap, not a coverage ceiling.

[READ, grounding thesis] For the CORRECTNESS/grounding thesis this is a substrate-readiness result: the DEFINE op can decide the definitional propositions the kernel has authored, on the covered (biomedical onto-obo) domain, for 77% of the minted corpus and ~100% of GO+SO. It strengthens the claim that the kernel HAS a decidable definitional substrate to ground against — a necessary precondition for any downstream grounding/verifier win — without itself being a grounding win over any baseline (no model, no external item, no null in this census).
does_not_license: [NOT kappa_B] 0.7710 is INTERNAL define-checkability of SELF-AUTHORED minted records; it is NOT N1-LB kappa_B and must never be quoted as one. kappa_B (architecture-ladder.md SS10.1) is the fraction of EXTERNAL benchmark items the define predicate makes checkable, and requires the binding THIRD leg — a mapper parse of a real benchmark stem into the closed query grammar (coverage-growth-ingestion-plan.md SS0). This census measures legs 1-2 (record present + define-op resolves) on the substrate; leg 3 is untouched. The governing MEASURED warning stands verbatim: d-ext showed ~49% lemma-touch coexisting with 0% checkability — vocabulary/substrate coverage and item checkability are different quantities, and checkability is the binding one.

[NOT an end-task claim] No accuracy, no baseline, no null, no model is in this census. It says the substrate is decidable, NOT that grounding the substrate helps any task. Any grounding-thesis end-task claim lives in the f2-line / N1-LB experiments, not here.

[ENVELOPE-BOUND] Domain-skewed and biomedical: the define-checkable slice is onto-obo biomedical (GO/SO/MONDO). Per the define-op doc (docs/design-kot-query-define-op.md), def-MMLU is largely NOT biomedical, so the define-checkable YIELD on a general benchmark 'may be small or ~0'. The selection-effect + biomedical-domain disclosure is mandatory on any downstream number; 0.7710 does not extrapolate to any benchmark or to the general concept space.

[EXTRAPOLATION, not a premise] 'Wave-A ingestion (HPO/ChEBI/NCBITaxon) pushes MONDO toward GO/SO-level completeness, and internal define-checkability toward ~1.0' is the mechanically-named DIRECTION on the Axis-A substrate axis, but the magnitude and the exact residual after ingestion are unmeasured (foreign-filler references can themselves reference further un-ingested terms). load_bearing:false; resolution_path = re-run define_census.py after each Wave-A shard lands. It does NOT translate to a kappa_B trajectory (that needs leg-3 + b-cov over benchmark items).

[SEPARATION] Endorsement was licensed by SHARD MEMBERSHIP (subject field non-load-bearing for THIS count, per RUN-LOG boundary-stop #1). The production definitional endorsement subject convention is UNPINNED (staged records carry __PENDING_FABLE_SUBJECT_PIN__, fail-closed). This census's counts are valid as a define-op resolvability measurement; they do NOT presuppose the eventual subject convention. Pinning it is a design choice — boundary-stop.
interpretation.expected_pattern_read: YES — on-trajectory, not a surprise. The coverage-growth-ingestion-plan predicted every observed cell: (1) the genus-differentia define-op is the cheapest kappa_B lever 'with the most existing substrate', GO's logical defs the named ready set => GO measured 1.0; (2) checkability is three coinciding legs, ingestion supplies legs 1-2 not leg 3 => this internal number is high while kappa_B stays unmeasured; (3) foreign-filler / cross-ontology references are the ingestion gap held to Wave-A/B => MONDO's exact 3,941 residual is HP/CHEBI/NCBITaxon. 8es converting the pre-8es 0.55 to 0.77 by resolving differentia-relation shorthands is the plan's 'exploits already-ingested logicalDefinition' lever behaving as designed.

trajectory_read.axis_a_internal_define_checkability: [EXTRAPOLATION, direction only] Toward ~1.0 as Wave-A completes: the 3,941 residual is entirely un-ingested foreign fillers with the named ingestion route (A4/A6/A7). Re-run the census after each shard lands to MEASURE the delta. This is the Axis-A substrate axis and it is realizable by ingestion alone (records-only), consistent with the plan's SS1 Axis-A characterisation.
trajectory_read.benchmark_kappa_b: [UNMEASURED, gated] Strictly separate and NOT implied by the internal number. kappa_B moves only with (i) the mapper parsing real WiC / def-MMLU stems into the define query (leg 3), (ii) the b-cov census (architecture-ladder.md SS10.1) run over EXTERNAL benchmark items, and (iii) the WiC/def-MMLU data unblock (WiC 401-gated; def-MMLU unpinned — RUN-LOG boundary-stop #2). Internal substrate completeness is necessary-not-sufficient for kappa_B; the LB-GATE thresholds (kappa_B >= 0.10 headline / >= 0.20 banner-free) are evaluated on b-cov output over benchmark items, never estimated from this census.
trajectory_read.what_this_census_unlocks: [READ] It makes the DEFINE-op substrate READY for a b-cov run against the Tier-A lexical/definitional benchmarks (WiC, def-MMLU) — it converts 'is the define-op lane functional on our records?' from open to MEASURED-yes (0.77, GO/SO complete). The next informative step is not more internal counting; it is the leg-3 / b-cov measurement over external items, which is a DIFFERENT census and is blocked on the benchmark-data unblock + the mapper-parse leg.

forks_resolved.fork: is the define-op engine the bottleneck, or the alias/relation-resolution layer?
forks_resolved.resolution: RESOLVED — the relation-resolution layer (bead 8es), not the engine: 8es moved 0.5478 -> 0.7710 with the engine byte-unchanged on GO; the residual is foreign-filler ingestion, not engine refusal. 0 failures on unminted genus/filler.

forks_resolved.fork: does internal substrate completeness imply benchmark kappa_B?
forks_resolved.resolution: NO — three-legs discipline (SS0): this census measures legs 1-2 on self-authored records; leg 3 (mapper parse of external items) is the binding, untouched quantity. Kept strictly separate.
YES — every cell (GO 1.0, SO unlocked, MONDO foreign-filler residual) was predicted by the ingestion plan; on-trajectory.

questions_opened.question: Re-run define_census.py after each Wave-A shard lands (A4 HPO, A6 ChEBI, A7 NCBITaxon) to MEASURE the internal define-checkability delta as the foreign-filler gap closes; the trajectory toward ~1.0 is currently EXTRAPOLATION.

The actually-informative next step for kappa_B: run the b-cov benchmark-checkability census (architecture-ladder.md SS10.1) with the define-op predicate over EXTERNAL WiC / def-MMLU items (gold-parse and mapper-parse counted separately). This is leg 3 and is a DIFFERENT census from this one; it is the only thing that yields a kappa_B number.
blocking: N1-LB kappa_B on the lexical Tier-A set

questions_opened.question: MAINTAINER/DESIGN pin needed: the production definitional endorsement subject convention (minted corpus-marker URN vs concept-URN). Staged records fail-closed on __PENDING_FABLE_SUBJECT_PIN__; Opus finalizes to data/axioms-v0/defn-onto-obo-{go,so,mondo}.json only after the pin. Not improvised here (boundary-stop; RUN-LOG boundary-stop #1).

MAINTAINER/DESIGN: the N1-LB kappa_B census instrument (SS10.1) needs a Fable instrument design + WiC/def-MMLU data (WiC 401-gated; def-MMLU unpinned) — NOT buildable by adapting m0b (RUN-LOG boundary-stop #2, bead kernel-of-truth-hu10). Boundary-stop.

_questions_note: est_cost_usd / est_agent_days are STIPULATED dry-plan estimates, never measurements. define-census-wave-a-rerun and b-cov-define-slice are Tier-0 CPU-only and now-eligible modulo the data unblock; define-subject-pin and kappa-b-instrument-design are maintainer/design decisions.

All 3,941 MONDO misses are un-ingested foreign fillers (HP/CHEBI/NCBITaxon); 0 engine failures on minted genus/filler.
ledger_updates.scope: onto-obo shards go 9d661d25 / so 10fae0e4 / mondo b9d20d63; DEFINE op post-8es; SELF-AUTHORED substrate, NOT benchmark items, NOT kappa_B

ledger_updates.statement: 8es (differentia-relation resolution + engine reading the resolved relation field) moved internal define-checkability 0.5478 -> 0.7710; SO 0.0822 -> 1.0, MONDO 0.0134 -> 0.4871; GO byte-unchanged. The engine was never the bottleneck; the alias/relation-resolution layer was.

This census gives NO kappa_B and NO end-task grounding claim: it measures checkability legs 1-2 on self-authored records; the binding leg 3 (mapper parse of external benchmark items) is untouched. d-ext's 49% lemma-touch / 0% checkability warning stands — substrate coverage != item checkability.

boundary_stops.reason: Unspecified by the memo SS3.1; staged records fail-closed on __PENDING_FABLE_SUBJECT_PIN__. A design pin, not an interpretation — not improvised here. Opus finalizes to data/axioms-v0/defn-onto-obo-{go,so,mondo}.json only after the pin.

boundary_stops.item: N1-LB kappa_B census instrument design + WiC/def-MMLU data
boundary_stops.reason: Needs a Fable instrument design + benchmark data (WiC 401-gated; def-MMLU unpinned); NOT buildable by adapting m0b (bead kernel-of-truth-hu10). The kappa_B trajectory cannot be measured until this is designed and the data unblocked.

tree_impact: genus-differentia define-op (Axis-A substrate) mini-tree: ALIVE and substrate-READY — internal define-checkability MEASURED at 0.7710 (GO/SO complete), the cheapest-kappa_B-lever's substrate lane is functional. Axis-A (linter): substrate 77% live, records-only value banked, residual has a named ingestion route. Axis-B / N1-LB kappa_B: UNCHANGED and still gated — this internal number is NOT kappa_B; the next informative move is the b-cov census over external items (leg 3), which is blocked on the benchmark-data unblock + mapper-parse leg (boundary-stop). Wave-A ingestion (A4 HPO / A6 ChEBI / A7 NCBITaxon) is the named route to close the MONDO residual; re-run the census after each shard to measure the delta. Endorsement-subject pin and the kappa_B instrument design are maintainer/design boundary-stops, not improvised. No verdict object exists (Tier-0 substrate census); this record is interpretation. Coordinator action (out of my scope): the interpretive-status tracker update is the coordinator's — I did not touch audit-status.jsonl.
